humanities_role_explanation: Designed for linguistic understanding of idiomatic expressions, targeting a core language and linguistics phenomenon
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal benchmark combining text context sentences with static images and temporal image sequences. Images are generated to represent literal vs idiomatic meanings of expressions, with captions provided for text-only evaluation.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Tasks require ranking images by alignment with idiomatic/literal meanings in context, predicting sequence completions, and distinguishing between literal and figurative interpretations - all requiring deep semantic understanding rather than surface pattern matching

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specialized benchmark focused specifically on idiomatic expression understanding in multimodal contexts

evaluates_generative_llm.note: GPT-4, QWEN2.5, DeepSeek, LLaVA, and other large language and vision-language models

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs and VLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for idiomaticity representation

arts_humanities_data.note: Language and Linguistics - focuses on idiomatic expressions, multi-word expressions, and figurative language understanding

quantitative_metrics.note: Top Image Accuracy, Discounted Cumulative Gain (DCG), Image completion accuracy, Sentence type accuracy

english_language.note: Paper is in English, benchmark includes both English and Brazilian Portuguese data

summary: This paper presents AdMIRe, a multimodal benchmark for evaluating how well language models understand idiomatic expressions through visual representation tasks. The benchmark includes two subtasks: ranking images by their alignment with literal vs idiomatic meanings, and predicting the next image in sequences depicting idiomatic expressions.
key_findings: Top-performing systems achieved human-level performance (93% accuracy) by leveraging mixture-of-experts approaches with multiple LLM queries, though this required substantial computational resources and prompt engineering to overcome models' bias toward literal interpretations.
